svg.title: Harmonic oscillator eigenstates — Hermite polynomial node count n=0 to 3
svg.desc: Four panels sharing a horizontal baseline axis showing the quantum harmonic oscillator wavefunctions for n=0 (zero nodes, single Gaussian peak), n=1 (one node at origin, odd function), n=2 (two nodes, even function with three lobes), and n=3 (three nodes, odd function with four lobes). Gaussian envelope shown as dashed orange reference curve. Positive lobes filled green, negative lobes filled red at 30% opacity.

cajal:figure.book: Quantum Mechanics — Math for Quantum Physics
cajal:figure.figure-title: Harmonic oscillator eigenstates — Hermite polynomial node count (n=0 to 3)
cajal:figure.author: Humanitarians AI Incorporated

svg.text: Node count = n: the nth eigenstate has exactly n zeros — a direct consequence of the Sturm oscillation theorem
All curves computed from ψₙ(x) = Hₙ(x)e^{−x²/2} with normalization; Gaussian envelope bounds all lobes
Even n → even functions; odd n → odd functions about x = 0
